{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent identifies and explains the logical inconsistency between the claimed predictive skill and the actual inter-model agreement.",
    "R1.3": "The agent audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure, flags the claim that the evidence does not support (the headline finding), and provides a detailed explanation.",
    "RX.1": "The agent marks uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the headline finding).",
    "RX.3": "Mostly within scope with one drift (exclusion of two regions to inflate the mean skill).",
    "RX.4": "Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (inflated headline claim).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.1,
    "RX.5": 0.1
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent identifies and explains the logical inconsistency between the claimed predictive skill and the actual inter-model agreement.
rationale.R1.3: The agent audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure, flags the claim that the evidence does not support (the headline finding), and provides a detailed explanation.
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the headline finding).
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with one drift (exclusion of two regions to inflate the mean skill).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (inflated headline claim).
